Our Accreditations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

About Our Teen Residential Treatment Program in Castle Rock, Colorado

When your teen is struggling with their mental health, it’s essential to choose the right level of care.

Our Castle Rock Residential Treatment Program offers a safe, structured setting designed specifically for teens to stabilize and begin healing. This 30-45 day program provides evidence-based treatment for anxiety, depression, trauma, and more , led by licensed clinicians and providers who specialize in adolescent care.

Your teen will follow an individualized treatment plan, participate in group therapy with peers, and build skills in a supportive environment. Family involvement is critical to recovery, which is why our programming includes regular family therapy . We help your teen lay the foundation for emotional wellness — while supporting your family throughout the process.

As part of Sandstone Care’s full continuum of care, your teen can step down into one of our outpatient programs. With three Colorado locations and additional centers nationwide, we provide ongoing support after residential treatment.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
How We Are Different

Sandstone Care aims to drive positive lasting change in real people. We understand that you have unique needs, so we offer a more personalized residential care experience than you would find anywhere else.

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Age-Specific Care

We are age-specific, not gender-specific. Our treatment is designed to fit your unique circumstances.

 

Evidence-Based Therapy

Our treatment interventions have been scientifically proven to provide consistent, lasting recovery.

 

Small Group Sizes

You are more than a number, which is why we provide an intimate setting where your voice can be heard.

 

Individualized Treatment Plans

Each person is unique, and so is their recovery journey. Your treatment program will be tailored to your needs.

 

Academic and Vocational Support

We want you to get the most out of life, which means feeling your best in the classroom or at your job.

 

The Family Component

Recovery involves the entire family, so we provide support for the whole family system.

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

Would highly recommend for anyone with a teen struggling with mental health.

We have felt included and supported and are so grateful for the care they’ve provided to our son at such a vulnerable and scary time.

 

 

 

 
Cindy G.

Parent
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Explore Adolescent Residential Treatment in Castle Rock

Our residential treatment center in Castle Rock, Colorado provides a safe, therapeutic environment for teens to focus on improving their mental health.

With a multidisciplinary team of licensed clinicians and staff, your teen will take part in a full schedule of evidence-based care — including individual and group therapy sessions, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectic Behavioral Therapy (DBT), and more — all designed to teach teens healthy coping skills and encourage long-term emotional wellness.

Each day includes nourishing, chef-prepared meals and meaningful activities that support both mental and physical wellbeing. Our Residential Treatment Program offers a balance of therapeutic structure and engaging experiences for young people, including recreational therapy, experiential therapies, and ongoing family therapy to strengthen support at home.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 

 

Tour the Residential Mental Health Center at Castle Rock

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Getting Started is Easy!

 

 

 

 

 
 

Step 1: Call Us

Call and speak with an admissions coordinator

 

 
 

Step 2: Verify Insurance

Verify insurance or set up a financial plan

 

 
 

Step 3: Schedule an Assessment

Schedule a clinical assessment to start treatment

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Residential Mental Health Treatment Center Reviews

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Benefits of Residential Mental Health Treatment

 

 

 

 

 

Safe, Secure Setting

Our Castle Rock Residential Mental Health Treatment Center was designed specifically for vulnerable populations and prioritizes safety, security, and comfort. The facility offers spacious common areas, calming therapy spaces, and a structured environment where your teen can focus on recovery — away from the stressors of daily life.

 

Professional Supervision

Your teen’s wellbeing is our priority. Our experienced clinical team offers 24/7 support in a secure, compassionate setting. With a low staff-to-client ratio, we’re able to provide personalized attention and consistent care — helping your teen build trust, develop skills, and make meaningful progress.

 

Length of Stay

The typical length of stay is 30–45 days. During that time, your teen will receive a personalized treatment plan and, as they prepare to transition, a customized aftercare plan. This ensures continued support — whether they return home or step down into outpatient care.

 

Chef Prepared Meals

We provide balanced, chef-prepared meals that support both physical health and emotional wellness. Our in-house chefs can accommodate all dietary needs and preferences, ensuring your teen is nourished in a way that promotes stability, energy, and a successful recovery process.

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

Alumni Testimonial – Nick’s Inspiring Journey

 https://vimeo.com/936249584 

From Chaos To Community

 “As someone who has spent all of my life – up until the complete breakdown – ignoring my mental health, there’s no time like the present to start working on yourself.” 

When Nick came to Sandstone, he’d been in and out of mental health programs with little to no success. He pinned his last hopes on Sandstone Care, and he hoped it would help him overcome his challenges for good.

What he got was just that, and so much more. Listen to Nick’s inspiring story of how the program helped him understand his mental health disorders, develop compassion for himself, and build a community of friends and mentors.

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
What We Treat

Within our Residential Program in Castle Rock, Colorado, we treat a number of mental health conditions and co-occurring disorders.

Our experienced clinicians will guide your teen through evidence-based treatment and holistic therapy so they can find lasting mental wellness, no matter what mental health issues they’re navigating.

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Depression Treatment

Depression involves persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and a lack of interest in daily activities.

 

Anxiety Treatment

Anxiety disorders are thought patterns of fear, dread, and uneasiness that affect other aspects of your life.

 

Bipolar Treatment

Bipolar disorder involves cycles of manic episodes with high energy and depressive episodes with sadness and low energy.

 

Trauma & PTSD Treatment

Trauma is the emotional response to a specific event like an accident, sexual abuse, or a natural disaster and can result in post-traumatic stress disorder.

 

 Attention Disorder Treatment

Attention disorders, such as ADHD, are marked by difficulty focusing, controlling impulsive behaviors, and over-activity.

 

Borderline Personality Disorder Treatment

Borderline Personality Disorder is characterized by unstable relationships and emotions, often leading to impulsive behavior and intense mood swings.

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
In Network With Most Major Insurance

 Instantly Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Explore Residential Mental Health Services

 

 

 

 
 
 

Individual Therapy
 
 

Group Therapy
 
 

Family Therapy
 
 

CBT
 
 

DBT
 
 

ACT
 

 

Individual Therapy

In our in-person therapy sessions, therapists help teenagers work through mood disorders one-on-one, and develop their own values, strengths, and passions.

 

Group Therapy

During daily support groups, your teen will interact with people in your age group, build healthy friendships, and see firsthand that they are not alone in their struggles.

 

Family Therapy

In family therapy, a therapist helps family members recognize the roles they play within emotional family dynamics, and learn how they can best support your teen.

 

CBT

Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) is one of a few talk therapy modalities that has been scientifically proven to improve mental health outcomes by guiding changes in thinking and behavioral patterns.

 

DBT

Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) is a type of talk therapy that is based on CBT, but it’s specially adapted for people who experience emotions very intensely, teaching teens healthier ways to cope.

 

ACT

Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) teaches teens to accept your thoughts and feelings while learning to recognize the difference between helpful and unhelpful mental experiences, leading to better emotional awareness and growth.

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

Your Teen Deserves Treatment That Works

You may have felt helpless watching your teen lose the ability to enjoy things like they used to. Something needs to change , but it can seem like everything you try to do to reach them only makes things worse.

In a recent study, our clients who completed treatment reported a 48% decrease in anxiety symptoms and a 63% decrease in symptoms of depression.

At Sandstone Care, we understand that sending your child to a residential treatment center is not something done on a whim. Which is why we track our outcomes closely, so you can feel confident that treatment is not just a shot in the dark, it’s an evidence-based, proven step towards a better life.

 Read Full Treatment Outcomes 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
The Continuum of Care

Care for Wherever You Are in Your Journey

Access a full range of treatments for mental health and substance use disorders. Whether you need a safe transitional living community, inpatient treatment, or outpatient therapy, we have a program to help.

 Learn more about levels of care 

 

 

 

 
 
 

Medical Detox and Inpatient
 
 

Residential Treatment
 
 

Day Treatment (PHP)
 
 

Intensive Outpatient (IOP)
 

 

Medical Detox and Inpatient

5-21 days of 24/7 on-site medical supervision.

Our Medically-Assisted Detox and Inpatient Center offers private rooms and 24/7 medically supervised care to support a safe recovery from drugs and alcohol, followed by comprehensive treatment that addresses your physical, psychological, emotional, and spiritual needs.

 

 
 

 

Medical Detox Explained 

 

 

Residential Treatment

30-45 days of on-site 24/7 treatment.

We provide residential treatment centers (RTCs) for teens (13-18) and young adults (18-35), treating mental health challenges including anxiety, depression, trauma, and substance use.

Our teen RTCs treat adolescents in a serene setting with smaller group sizes and a focus on strengthening the family unit. The length of stay is 30-45 days. Our young adult RTC offers young adults a peaceful environment in which they can be treated for mental health challenges within 30-45 days. Both RTCs create a distraction-free space for healing, helping clients return to daily life feeling more balanced and well.

 

 
 

 

Teen Residential Care Explained 

 

 

Day Treatment (PHP)

4 weeks of on-site day treatment.

Our Teen & Young Adult Day Treatment program, also known as Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP), is a highly structured level of care that offers five days of robust programming a week.

Our mental health and substance use programming helps clients to stabilize, begin to understand their mental health and/or addiction struggles, and heal from them. We strive to help our clients become more like the person they want to be, without using negative coping strategies or substances to get there.

 

 
 

 

PHP Explained 

 

 

Intensive Outpatient (IOP)

12 weeks of on-site or virtual treatment.

Our Teen & Young Adult IOP, or Intensive Outpatient Program, consists of 3-4 days of weekly programming that treats mental health challenges such as depression, trauma, anxiety, and substance use.

IOP includes individual therapy, group therapy, and family therapy. This flexible treatment option occurs in the evening hours so you can continue meeting your school or work responsibilities while receiving care.

 

 
 

 

What is IOP?

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
TEAM

Our Leadership Team

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Brad Owens

 Chief Executive Officer 

 

 

 

Dr. Robert Ochsner, MD

 Chief Medical Officer 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Questions About Residential Treatment?

Our goal is to provide you with the most helpful information. Please reach out to us if you have any additional questions at (888) 850-1890 . We are here to help in any way we can.

 

 

 

 
 
 Collapse All -->
 Expand All -->
 
 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 
How do I know if my teen needs residential addiction or mental health treatment?

 
 
 

 
If your teen is still struggling to manage their mental health despite outpatient therapy or medication, or if their ability to function day-to-day is significantly impacted, residential treatment may be the next step toward healing.

Some signs that residential care may be appropriate include difficulty maintaining relationships, falling behind at school, persistent feelings of hopelessness, thoughts of self-harm, or suicidal ideation.

At our Residential Treatment facility in Colorado, we provide comprehensive support for both addiction and mental health care in a structured, teen-focused setting. With 24/7 care from licensed clinicians and a peaceful environment designed for healing, your teen will receive the personalized attention they need to begin meaningful recovery.

The best way to determine whether residential care is right for your teen is to consult with a mental health professional. You can also speak directly with our admissions team — we’ll listen to your concerns, answer your questions, and help you take the next step with clarity and support.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What should my teen bring to the Residential Treatment Program in Castle Rock, Colorado? 

 
 
 

 
Our admissions team can walk you through everything your teen will need. We’ll provide a detailed packing list and answer any questions to help you feel fully prepared. Call (888) 850-1890 to get started.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
Who will be providing treatment? 

 
 
 

 
Each teen’s care team is made up of licensed professionals who specialize in teen mental health and addiction treatment. This includes a Program Director, Registered Nurses, Therapists, Doctors, a Discharge Coordinator, Medication Provider, and Family Therapist — all working together to support your teen’s progress.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
Do you accept insurance?

 
 
 

 
Yes! We are in-network with most major providers. To check coverage, please complete our quick Insurance Verification form to confirm if we accept your plan.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
Can family and friends visit my teen during treatment?

 
 
 

 
To maintain a safe and focused treatment environment, we don’t allow in-person visits — but we do support virtual visits with family and close friends. These can be scheduled throughout your teen’s stay to maintain healthy, connected support.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
Do you use medications to assist with treatment? 

 
 
 

 
Yes, we offer medication management services as part of our Castle Rock Residential Treatment Program . These services help ensure that your teen stays on track with any prescribed medications. Please note that we do not offer medication-assisted treatment (MAT), which is typically reserved for detox settings.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
How does Sandstone Care treat the family?

 
 
 

 
Connection is key in recovery, and loved ones play a vital role in behavioral health and the overall wellness of a person. That’s why we offer individual family therapy sessions every other week. These sessions help family members understand how their role influences recovery — and how to create new, healthier patterns.

We also offer weekly Multi- Family therapy groups to reinforce key skills, strengthen communication, and give families a source of guidance and support throughout the treatment journey.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
How do I start the process of entering my teen into Sandstone Care treatment?

 
 
 

 
You can begin by calling our Admissions Team at (888) 850-1890 . We’ll walk you through every step of getting started with our Teen Residential Treatment Program , answer your questions, and help determine if it’s the right fit for your teen.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Sandstone Care Teen Center At Castle Rock

We help teens and their families overcome challenges with mental health including anxiety, depression, trauma, and addiction in Castle Rock, Colorado.

 

 Address: 864 Barranca Drive, Castle Rock, CO 80104, United States 

 Phone: (720) 782-0502 

 Hours: 24 Hours

 Levels of Care: Teen Residential, Assessments

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
Let’s Take the Next Steps Together

We understand taking the first step is difficult. There is no shame or guilt in asking for help or more information. We are here to support you in any way we can.

 

 
 Call (888) 850-1890 Verify Insurance 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 Verify Insurance (888) 850-1890

class="image-text__content"><h2>Young Adult Addiction Treatment Centers</h2>
<p>We help young adults overcome substance dependency, address mental health concerns that may be causing substance misuse, and restore relationships that have been damaged by addiction.</p>
<p>Because we create treatment plans in context of your work, life, family, and mental health needs, our comprehensive and individualized methods help you achieve the highest rate of success in recovery.</p>
<p>Your substance addiction can be treated, and you can get better. Find hope and a path to a better tomorrow at one of our recovery centers.</p>
<p><a class="cta-link" href="https://www.sandstonecare.com/locations/">See Locations</a></p>

					                            <div class="l-blurb__col list-item-column intro_copy  col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 ">
                                <div class='blurb js-blurb'>
                                    <div class='blurb__text-container'>
                                        <h2>How We Are Different</h2>
<p>Sandstone Care aims to drive positive lasting change in real people. We understand that you have a life, so we offer a more personalized experience than you would find anywhere else.</p>
<p><a class="btn-yellow" href="tel:8888501890">Call (888) 850-1890</a> <a class="btn-primary" href="/insurance/">Verify Insurance</a></p>

						<div class="l-blurb__col list-item-column blurb_column  col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 "><article class="icon_image_small blurb js-blurb blurb--default_column_style "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image_small'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="Age-Specific Care" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AgeSpecificCare_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AgeSpecificCare_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="Age-Specific Care" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Age-Specific Care</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p>We are age-specific, not gender-specific. We focus on the unique circumstances that young adults face.</p>
</div></article><article class="icon_image_small blurb js-blurb blurb--default_column_style "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image_small'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="Evidence-Based Therapy" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_EvidenceBasedTherapy_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_EvidenceBasedTherapy_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="Evidence-Based Therapy" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Evidence-Based Therapy</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p>We only use treatment interventions that have been scientifically proven to provide consistent, lasting recovery. </p>
</div></article><article class="icon_image_small blurb js-blurb blurb--default_column_style "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image_small'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="Small Group Sizes" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_SmallyGroupSizes_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_SmallyGroupSizes_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="Small Group Sizes" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Small Group Sizes</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p>You are not a number, that’s why we provide a comfortable setting where your voice can be heard.  </p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-blurb__col list-item-column blurb_column  col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 "><article class="icon_image_small blurb js-blurb blurb--default_column_style "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image_small'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="Individualized Treatment Plans" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_IndividualizedTreatmentPlan_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_IndividualizedTreatmentPlan_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="Individualized Treatment Plans" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Individualized Treatment Plans</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p>No one person is the same, and neither is their recovery journey. Your treatment program will be tailored to your unique needs.</p>
</div></article><article class="icon_image_small blurb js-blurb blurb--default_column_style "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image_small'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="Academic and Vocational Support" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AcademicAndVocationalSupport_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AcademicAndVocationalSupport_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="Academic and Vocational Support" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Academic and Vocational Support</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p>We want clients to get the most out of life, which means doing their best in the classroom or at their job.</p>
</div></article><article class="icon_image_small blurb js-blurb blurb--default_column_style "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image_small'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="The Family Component" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_TheFamilyComponent_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_TheFamilyComponent_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="The Family Component" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>The Family Component</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p>Recovery involves the entire family, so we provide support for the whole family system.</p>

            <div class="gutenblock-header__content text-center">
                <h2 style="text-align: center;">Drug & Alcohol Treatment Options</h2>
<p style="text-align: center;">Struggling with substance use or addiction? Our rehab centers can help.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><a class="btn-yellow" href="tel:8888501890">Call (888) 850-1890</a> <a class="btn-secondary" href="https://www.sandstonecare.com/insurance/">Verify Insurance</a></p>

                    <div class="l-columns__row row"><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-xl-3"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AlcoholTreatment_100422.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AlcoholTreatment_100422.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Alcohol</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">The transition between recreational alcohol use and alcohol dependence can be a subtle one. But if you’re finding that you can’t stop drinking when you want to, you may have alcohol dependence.</p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-xl-3"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_MarijuanaTreatment_100422.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_MarijuanaTreatment_100422.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Marijuana</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">Similar to alcohol use, smoking or ingesting marijuana has become normalized as a way to relax or take the edge off. When overused, it can make you feel unable to cope with life without using.</p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-xl-3"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_StimulantTreatment_100422.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_StimulantTreatment_100422.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Stimulants</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">A stimulant is any drug that increases your central nervous system activity. Stimulants are used to heighten attention, hyperactivity, and alertness. Abuse of these drugs can lead to a variety of health problems.</p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-xl-3"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_OpiodTreatment_100422.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_OpiodTreatment_100422.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Opioids</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">Opioids are a class of drugs that include synthetic opioids, pain killers, and the illegal drug heroin. These drugs, including prescription medications, are highly addictive.</p>
</div></article></div></div>

l-image-text__col--text list-item-column col-12 col-lg-6 order-2 order-lg-1"><div class="image-text__content"><h2>The Obvious Choice for Effective Treatment</h2>
<p>At Sandstone Care, we believe in personalized and specialized care. Unlike generic treatment approaches, we understand that each individual’s circumstances are unique.</p>
<p>Through thorough assessments, our licensed therapists, medical professionals, and experienced staff gain a deep understanding of your background, challenges, and goals. Comprehensive programming, therapy, and education, equips your family with the necessary tools to support your loved ones’ recovery journey.</p>
<p>In our treatment programs for young adults, you’ll receive the right level of support at each stage of recovery. Whether you need high-support care, or a few hours of outpatient treatment a week, our expert team will collaborate with you to find what works best for you.</p>
</div></div></div>                </div>

>Residential Treatment</h5><div class='row align-items-center justify-content-between'><div class='col-12 col-md-6 col-xl-5  text_column '><p class="intro-text">30-45 days of on-site 24/7 treatment.</p>
<p>We provide residential treatment centers (RTCs) for teens 13-17 (18 in MD if still in high school) and young adults 18-35, treating mental health challenges including anxiety, depression, trauma, and substance use.</p>
<p>Our teen RTCs treat adolescents in a serene setting with smaller group sizes and a focus on strengthening the family unit. The length of stay is 30-45 days. Our young adult RTC offers young adults a peaceful environment in which they can be treated for mental health challenges. Both RTCs create a distraction-free space for healing, helping clients return to daily life feeling more balanced and well.</p>
</div><div class='col-12 col-md-6  video_column mt-4 mt-md-0'>

What is Substance Use and Mental Health Rehab Like For Teens" class="video-iframe"  data-src="https://player.vimeo.com/video/890456825?dnt=1&app_id=122963" width="640" height="360" frameborder="0" allow="autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin"></iframe></div></div></div></div></div><div class='col-12 tab-section ' id='Day-Treatment--PHP-'><div class='tab-section__inner'><div class='tab-section__image-wrap'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="tab-section__image-wrap__icon" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/icon-sun-heart.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/icon-sun-heart.svg" class="tab-section__image-wrap__icon" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='link-container__title' >Day Treatment (PHP)</h5><div class='row align-items-center justify-content-between'><div class='col-12 col-md-6 col-xl-5  text_column '><p class="intro-text">4 weeks of on-site day treatment.</p>
<p>Our Teen & Young Adult Day Treatment program, also known as Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP), is a highly structured level of care that offers five days of robust programming a week.</p>
<p>Our mental health and substance use programming helps clients to stabilize, begin to understand their mental health and/or addiction struggles, and heal from them. We strive to help our clients become more like the person they want to be, without using negative coping strategies or substances to get there.</p>
</div><div class='col-12 col-md-6  video_column mt-4 mt-md-0'>

class='link-container__title' >Intensive Outpatient (IOP)</h5><div class='row align-items-center justify-content-between'><div class='col-12 col-md-6 col-xl-5  text_column '><p class="intro-text">12 weeks of on-site or virtual treatment.</p>
<p>Our Teen & Young Adult IOP, or Intensive Outpatient Program, consists of 3-4 days of weekly programming that treats mental health challenges such as depression, trauma, anxiety, and substance use.</p>
<p>IOP includes individual therapy, group therapy, and family therapy. This flexible treatment option occurs in the evening hours so you can continue meeting your school or work responsibilities while receiving care.</p>
</div><div class='col-12 col-md-6  video_column mt-4 mt-md-0'>

                    <div class="l-video-text__row row align-items-center justify-content-center"><div class="l-video-text__col l-video-text__col--text list-item-column col-12 col-lg-6 order-2 order-lg-0"><div class="image-text__content"><h2>Find Hope at Sandstone Care</h2>
<p class="intro-text;"><strong>“Sandstone helped me to dig deep… and it taught me how to have a relationship with myself and relationships with others again. I was able to take the mask off, and I don’t have to put it back on.”</strong></p>
<p>Watch Jesse detail how he took the leap to recover from drug use and find himself again. Discover the skills he learned during his time with Sandstone, including accountability for actions, self-sufficiency, and emotional regulation that allowed him to claim control over his life and stay sober.</p>
<p>By tailoring treatment to the unique needs of patients like Jesse, we create an environment that fosters growth, healing, and the development of a strong support network.</p>

    <div class="row gutenblock-header justify-content-center">
        <div class="col-12 col-sm-10 col-lg-8 l-gutenblock-header__col">
            <div class="gutenblock-header__content text-center">
                <h2 style="text-align: center;">You Have Questions. We Have Answers.</h2>
<p style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Our goal is to provide the most helpful information. Please reach out to us at </span><a href="tel:8888501890"><span style="font-weight: 400;">(888) 850-1890</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> if you have any additional questions. We are here to help in any way we can.</span></p>
            </div>
        </div>

<!--                <div class="row expand_collapse_row">-->
<!--                    <div class="expand_collapse_all_container">-->
<!--                        <span class="collapse_all">Collapse All</span>-->
<!--                        <span class="expand_all">Expand All</span>-->
<!--                    </div>-->
<!--                </div>-->
                <div class="row faq_row justify-content-center">
                    <div class="faq_column col-12 col-lg-8">

                        
                            <div class="faq_container">
                                <div class="faq_container_inner">
                                    <button type="button" class="faq_question" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="sect-1" id="accordion-1-id">
                                        <h3 class="faq_title">How much treatment do young adults need?</h3>
                                        <span class="faq_trigger"></span>
                                    </button>
                                    <div id="sect-1" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-1-id" class="faq_answer">
                                        <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The amount of treatment you will need varies depending on your individual needs. At Sandstone Care, we believe in treating you as a whole person instead of just treating your substance use disorder in isolation, which is why length of stay may vary.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Typically, you can expect the Young Adult IOP program to be 16 hours a week, from 8 to 12 weeks.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">After treatment, you will also have the opportunity to join support groups and other aftercare modalities to maintain the momentum you found in our drug addiction recovery program.</span></p>
                                    </div>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                                                    
                            <div class="faq_container">
                                <div class="faq_container_inner">
                                    <button type="button" class="faq_question" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="sect-2" id="accordion-2-id">
                                        <h3 class="faq_title">Do you use Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)?</h3>
                                        <span class="faq_trigger"></span>
                                    </button>
                                    <div id="sect-2" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-2-id" class="faq_answer">
                                        <p>Yes. Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT) is proven to be effective in treating substance use disorders, especially in combination with evidence-based therapy and counseling. Our personalized programming is designed to learn your needs and goals so we can choose the best course of treatment for your specific challenges. By offering medically-supervised withdrawal from substances, we help our clients recover quickly and safely from drug or alcohol use.</p>
<p>We also recognize that not all situations call for MAT. Our medical team will evaluate your circumstances and determine what type of treatment is appropriate for you. <a href="https://www.sandstonecare.com/treatments/medication-assisted-treatment/">Learn more about Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) here</a>.</p>
                                    </div>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                                                    
                            <div class="faq_container">
                                <div class="faq_container_inner">
                                    <button type="button" class="faq_question" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="sect-3" id="accordion-3-id">
                                        <h3 class="faq_title">What are the other people in young adult rehabs like? </h3>
                                        <span class="faq_trigger"></span>
                                    </button>
                                    <div id="sect-3" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-3-id" class="faq_answer">
                                        <p>The young adults at Sandstone Care are people who have been impacted by a variety of addiction and mental health issues. Typically they are struggling with or have struggled with anxiety, depression, trauma, and drug abuse.</p>
<p>The safety of our clients is our top priority, so individuals who exhibit behavioral issues that would disrupt the treatment milieu are not appropriate for Sandstone Care. This includes individuals with a history of violence, are actively suicidal or psychotic, or who have severe and persistent mental illness such as schizophrenia.</p>
<p>Each client is carefully screened by the admissions team and the Program Director before they enter the recovery center.</p>
                                    </div>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                                                    
                            <div class="faq_container">
                                <div class="faq_container_inner">
                                    <button type="button" class="faq_question" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="sect-4" id="accordion-4-id">
                                        <h3 class="faq_title">How do I know if I need detox/inpatient care?</h3>
                                        <span class="faq_trigger"></span>
                                    </button>
                                    <div id="sect-4" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-4-id" class="faq_answer">
                                        <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Knowing what level of care would be best for you is tricky! If you experience withdrawal symptoms when you attempt to discontinue use, it is more than likely you would benefit from detox. However, the best way to know whether or not you would benefit from medical detox is to get a professional opinion. Our trained admissions team would be happy to speak with you and advise you on next steps.</span></p>
                                    </div>
                                </div>
                            </div>
                                                    
                            <div class="faq_container">
                                <div class="faq_container_inner">
                                    <button type="button" class="faq_question" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="sect-5" id="accordion-5-id">
                                        <h3 class="faq_title">What is the most effective intervention for substance use?</h3>
                                        <span class="faq_trigger"></span>
                                    </button>
                                    <div id="sect-5" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-5-id" class="faq_answer">
                                        <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">There are a variety of different effective approaches and strategies for providing treatment for substance abuse problems.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Early intervention can help reduce the harm of substances, reduce risk behaviors, and improve health and social function. Health care that is age group specific and involves family members is also proven to work faster and last longer than other addiction treatment programs.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The most common and effective interventions for substance abuse have been shown to include a form of behavioral health therapy in order for you to learn coping skills.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Examples of mental health treatments can include Cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), Dialectical behavioral therapy (DBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT).</span></p>
                                    </div>
                                </div>
                            </div>

<script>
const url = new URL(window.location.href);
const params = url.searchParams;

// Normalize params to lowercase
const normalized = {};
for (const [key, value] of params.entries()) {
	normalized[key.toLowerCase()] = value;
}

const paramDefs = [
	{ name: 'gad_source', label: 'GAD Source' },
	{ name: 'gad_campaignid', label: 'GAD Campaign ID' },
	{ name: 'gbraid', label: 'GBRAID' },
	{ name: 'gclid', label: 'GCLID' },
	{ name: 'utm_source', label: 'UTM Source' },
	{ name: 'utm_medium', label: 'UTM Medium' },
	{ name: 'utm_campaign', label: 'UTM Campaign' },
	{ name: 'utm_content', label: 'UTM Content' },
	{ name: 'msclkid', label: 'MS Click ID' },
];

const queryData = paramDefs
	.filter(def => normalized[def.name] !== undefined)
	.map(def => ({
		name: def.name,
		label: def.label,
		type: 'string',
		data: normalized[def.name]
	}));

// Inject into Talkative config
window.talkativeCustomConfig = {
	events: {
		enterStandby() {
			if (window.talkativeApi?.interactionData && queryData.length > 0) {
				window.talkativeApi.interactionData.appendInteractionData(queryData);
			}
		}
	}
};

import(`https://us.engage.app/api/ecs/v1/loader/a9b2f753-a85e-4356-be2a-746e023b3b81.js?path=${encodeURIComponent(window.location.origin+window.location.pathname)}`)
</script>

<script type="rocketlazyloadscript" data-rocket-type="text/javascript">
function handleCheckboxChange() {
    const checkbox = document.querySelector('.sms_input input');
    const fieldValue = document.querySelector('.sms_field_value input');
    if (checkbox && fieldValue) {
        fieldValue.value = checkbox.checked ? 'true' : 'false';
    }
}

function initializeCheckboxHandler() {
    const checkbox = document.querySelector('.sms_input input');
    if (checkbox) {
        checkbox.addEventListener('change', handleCheckboxChange);
        handleCheckboxChange();
    }
}

function observeFormChanges() {
    const observer = new MutationObserver(() => {
        initializeCheckboxHandler();
    });
    const form = document.querySelector('form');
    if (form) {
        observer.observe(form, { childList: true, subtree: true });
    }
}

initializeCheckboxHandler();
observeFormChanges();

</script>

<script type="rocketlazyloadscript" data-rocket-type="text/javascript">
function getParameterByName(name, url = window.location.href) {
	name = name.replace(/[\[\]]/g, '\\$&');
	var regex = new RegExp('[?&]' + name + '(=([^&#]*)|&|#|$)');
	var results = regex.exec(url);
	if (!results) return null;
	if (!results[2]) return '';
	return decodeURIComponent(results[2].replace(/\+/g, ' '));
}

function setCookie(name, value, days) {
	let expires = "";
	if (days) {
		let date = new Date();
		date.setTime(date.getTime() + days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
		expires = "; expires=" + date.toUTCString();
	}
	document.cookie = name + "=" + (value || "") + expires + "; path=/";
}

function getCookie(name) {
	let nameEQ = name + "=";
	let ca = document.cookie.split(';');
	for (let i = 0; i < ca.length; i++) {
		let c = ca[i].trim();
		if (c.indexOf(nameEQ) === 0) return c.substring(nameEQ.length, c.length);
	}
	return null;
}

function getUTMParameterOrCookie(paramName) {
	let paramValue = getParameterByName(paramName);
	if (paramValue) {
		setCookie(paramName, paramValue, 30); // Save in cookie for 30 days
	} else {
		paramValue = getCookie(paramName); // Get from cookie if not in URL
	}
	return paramValue;
}

function getTrafficSource() {
	var lookupTable = {
		"pinterest / cpc": "Pinterest Paid",
		"pinterest.com / referral": "Pinterest Organic",
		"google / cpc": "Google Adwords",
		"bing / cpc": "Bing Paid",
		"facebook / cpc": "Facebook Paid",
		"youtube.com / referral": "YouTube",
		"search.yahoo.com / referral": "Yahoo Organic",
		"GMBlisting / organic": "Organic Maps",
		"google.com / referral": "Google Organic",
		"psychologytoday.com / referral": "Psychology Today",
		"bing.com / referral": "Bing Organic",
		"facebook.com / banner": "Facebook",
		"m.facebook.com / referral": "Facebook",
		"facebook.com / referral": "Facebook",
		"l.facebook.com / referral": "Facebook",
		"lm.facebook.com / referral": "Facebook",
	};

	function determineTrafficSource() {
		var gclid = getUTMParameterOrCookie('gclid'); // Check gclid in URL or cookies
		var msclkid = getUTMParameterOrCookie('msclkid'); // Check msclkid in URL or cookies
		var utm_source = getUTMParameterOrCookie('utm_source');
		var utm_medium = getUTMParameterOrCookie('utm_medium');
		var referral = document.referrer;

		// If gclid exists, the traffic source is Google Adwords
		if (gclid) {
			return "Google Adwords";
		}

		var sourceKey = "";

		if (msclkid) {
			sourceKey = "bing / cpc";
		} else if (utm_source === "pinterest") {
			sourceKey = "pinterest / cpc";
		} else if (utm_source && utm_medium) {
			sourceKey = utm_source + " / " + utm_medium;
		} else if (utm_medium === "facebook") {
			sourceKey = "facebook / cpc";
		} else if (referral) {
			var referrerDomain = referral.split('/')[2].replace(/^www\./, '');
			sourceKey = referrerDomain + " / referral";
		} else {
			sourceKey = "";
		}

		return lookupTable[sourceKey] || "Website";
	}

	function getStoredTrafficSource() {
		var cookieMatch = getCookie('trafficSource');
		if (cookieMatch) {
			try {
				// Декодируем строку перед парсингом
				var storedSource = JSON.parse(decodeURIComponent(cookieMatch));
				return storedSource.source;
			} catch (error) {
				console.error("Error parsing trafficSource cookie:", error);
				return null;
			}
		}
		return null;
	}

	var detectedSource = determineTrafficSource();
	var storedSource = getStoredTrafficSource();

	// Update cookies if any UTM parameters or traffic source changes
	var sourceString = JSON.stringify({ source: detectedSource });
	if (storedSource !== detectedSource) {
		setCookie("trafficSource", sourceString, 90);
	}

	return detectedSource;
}

// Example usage
var trafficSource = getTrafficSource();
console.log("Traffic Source:", trafficSource);

// Function to handle GCLID
function handleGclid() {
	let gclid = getUTMParameterOrCookie('gclid');
	const forms = document.querySelectorAll('form');

	forms.forEach((form) => {
		const gclidField = form.querySelector('.GCLID input');
		if (gclidField) gclidField.value = gclid;
	});

	console.log("Handled GCLID:", gclid);
}

// Call handleGclid if traffic source is Google Adwords
if (trafficSource === "Google Adwords") {
	handleGclid();
}



// Fill UTM fields in forms
function fillFieldsInForms() {
	
	var utm_source = getUTMParameterOrCookie('utm_source');
	var utm_medium = getUTMParameterOrCookie('utm_medium');
	var utm_campaign = getUTMParameterOrCookie('utm_campaign');
	var utm_content = getUTMParameterOrCookie('utm_content');

	const forms = document.querySelectorAll('form');
	forms.forEach((form) => {
		const utmSrcField = form.querySelector('.SRC input');
		if (utmSrcField) utmSrcField.value = trafficSource;
		
		const utmSourceField = form.querySelector('.utm_source input');
		if (utmSourceField) utmSourceField.value = utm_source;

		const utmMediumField = form.querySelector('.utm_medium input');
		if (utmMediumField) utmMediumField.value = utm_medium;

		const utmCampaignField = form.querySelector('.utm_campaign input');
		if (utmCampaignField) utmCampaignField.value = utm_campaign;

		const utmContentField = form.querySelector('.utm_content input');
		if (utmContentField) utmContentField.value = utm_content;
	});

	// Log all UTM parameters and GCLID
	console.log("UTM Source:", utm_source);
	console.log("UTM Medium:", utm_medium);
	console.log("UTM Campaign:", utm_campaign);
	console.log("UTM Content:", utm_content);
	console.log("GCLID:", getUTMParameterOrCookie('gclid'));
}

fillFieldsInForms();

</script>
<!-- end Simple Custom CSS and JS -->

<script type="rocketlazyloadscript" data-rocket-type="text/javascript">
const links = document.querySelectorAll('.block_anchor_link');

if (links.length > 0) {
	links.forEach(link => {
		link.addEventListener('click', function (event) {
			event.preventDefault();
			event.stopImmediatePropagation();

			const blockRefId = this.getAttribute('data-block_ref_id');
			if (blockRefId) {
				const allLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.block_anchor_link');
				allLinks.forEach(item => {
					item.classList.remove('active');
				});
				
				this.classList.add('active');
				const targetElement = document.querySelector(blockRefId);
				if (targetElement) {
					const heading = targetElement.querySelector('h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6');
					const scrollTarget = heading || targetElement;
	
					const targetPosition = scrollTarget.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY;

					let offset = 145;

					if(window.matchMedia('(max-width: 768px)').matches) {
						offset = 170;
					}

					//console.log(offset);

					window.scrollTo({
						top: targetPosition - offset,
						behavior: 'smooth'
					});
					document.querySelector('#jumplink-nav').classList.remove('show');
					document.querySelector('#post-nav-trigger').classList.remove('open');
				}
			}
		});
	});
}


(() => {
	const menuItems = document.querySelectorAll(".footer-menu-accordeon .menu-item-has-children > a");

	menuItems.forEach(link => {
		link.addEventListener("click", function (e) {
			e.preventDefault(); 

			const parentItem = this.parentElement;

// 			document.querySelectorAll(".footer-menu-accordeon .menu-item-has-children").forEach(item => {
// 				if (item !== parentItem) {
// 					item.classList.remove("open");
// 				}
// 			});

			parentItem.classList.toggle("open");
		});
	});
})();

</script>

<script type="rocketlazyloadscript" data-rocket-type="text/javascript">
(() => {
	     function loadScriptWhenInView(entries, observer) {
        entries.forEach(entry => {
            if (entry.isIntersecting) {
                let scriptTag = entry.target.querySelector('script[data-src]');
                if (scriptTag) {
                    // Move data-src to src to load the script
                    let dataSrc = scriptTag.getAttribute('data-src');
                    scriptTag.setAttribute('src', dataSrc);
                    scriptTag.removeAttribute('data-src');
                    
                    // Optionally, stop observing once the script is loaded
                    observer.unobserve(entry.target);
                }
            }
        });
    }

    // Create an IntersectionObserver
    let observer = new IntersectionObserver(loadScriptWhenInView, {
        root: null, // Use the viewport as root
        rootMargin: '200px 0px', // Trigger 200px before the element enters the viewport
        threshold: 0 // Trigger as soon as any part of the element is in view
    });

    // Target the container block with class 'form-columns__form-wrap'
    let targetBlocks = document.querySelectorAll('.form-columns__form-wrap');
    
    // Observe each target block
	if (targetBlocks.length) {
		targetBlocks.forEach(block => {
			observer.observe(block);
		});
	}
   
})();
</script>

document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
  document.querySelectorAll('.iframe-wrapper.js-video').forEach((wrap) => {
    const overlay = wrap.querySelector('.video-overlay');
    const iframe = wrap.querySelector('iframe.video-iframe');
    if (!overlay || !iframe) return;

    overlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
      e.preventDefault();
      e.stopPropagation();

      const base = iframe.dataset.src;
      if (!base) return;

      const joiner = base.includes('?') ? '&' : '?';
      iframe.src = base + joiner + 'autoplay=1';

      overlay.style.display = 'none';
    }, true);
  });
});
</script>

 Home Page / Care for Young Adults / Substance Use for Young Adults 
 Care for Young Adults 
Rehab for Young Adults

Explore programming options designed to help young adults struggling with substance use disorder.

 
 

 

 
 

 

Young Adult Substance Use Treatment Overview

 

 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
We’re here to help 24/7

 

 

 (888) 850-1890 Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Our Accreditations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

Young Adult Addiction Treatment Centers

We help young adults overcome substance dependency, address mental health concerns that may be causing substance misuse, and restore relationships that have been damaged by addiction.

Because we create treatment plans in context of your work, life, family, and mental health needs, our comprehensive and individualized methods help you achieve the highest rate of success in recovery.

Your substance addiction can be treated, and you can get better. Find hope and a path to a better tomorrow at one of our recovery centers.

 See Locations 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
How We Are Different

Sandstone Care aims to drive positive lasting change in real people. We understand that you have a life, so we offer a more personalized experience than you would find anywhere else.

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Age-Specific Care

We are age-specific, not gender-specific. We focus on the unique circumstances that young adults face.

 

Evidence-Based Therapy

We only use treatment interventions that have been scientifically proven to provide consistent, lasting recovery. 

 

Small Group Sizes

You are not a number, that’s why we provide a comfortable setting where your voice can be heard. 

 

Individualized Treatment Plans

No one person is the same, and neither is their recovery journey. Your treatment program will be tailored to your unique needs.

 

Academic and Vocational Support

We want clients to get the most out of life, which means doing their best in the classroom or at their job.

 

The Family Component

Recovery involves the entire family, so we provide support for the whole family system.

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Getting Started is Easy!

 

 

 

 

 
 

Step 1: Call Us

Call and speak with an admissions coordinator

 

 
 

Step 2: Verify Insurance

Verify insurance or set up a financial plan

 

 
 

Step 3: Schedule an Assessment

Schedule a clinical assessment to start treatment

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
What Clients Are Saying

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

Robert’s Story

 

 “I had no idea what to expect for Sandstone, but it was everything I needed and more. Everybody (at Sandstone) really cares about what they’re doing and cares to see everybody get better.” 

 

 

 

 
Robert

Alumni
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

Alex’s Story

 

 “Things progressed where I was at the point that I wanted to die…My life now is very different after going through treatment at Sandstone…The people here, they really care.” 

 

 

 

 
Alex

Alumni
 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

Jesse’s Story

 

 “Sandstone helped me to dig deep… and it taught me how to have a relationship with myself and relationships with others again. I was able to take the mask off, and I don’t have to put it back on.” 

 

 

 

 
Jesse

Alumni
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Drug & Alcohol Treatment Options

Struggling with substance use or addiction? Our rehab centers can help.

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Alcohol

The transition between recreational alcohol use and alcohol dependence can be a subtle one. But if you’re finding that you can’t stop drinking when you want to, you may have alcohol dependence.

 

Marijuana

Similar to alcohol use, smoking or ingesting marijuana has become normalized as a way to relax or take the edge off. When overused, it can make you feel unable to cope with life without using.

 

Stimulants

A stimulant is any drug that increases your central nervous system activity. Stimulants are used to heighten attention, hyperactivity, and alertness. Abuse of these drugs can lead to a variety of health problems.

 

Opioids

Opioids are a class of drugs that include synthetic opioids, pain killers, and the illegal drug heroin. These drugs, including prescription medications, are highly addictive.

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

The Obvious Choice for Effective Treatment

At Sandstone Care, we believe in personalized and specialized care. Unlike generic treatment approaches, we understand that each individual’s circumstances are unique.

Through thorough assessments, our licensed therapists, medical professionals, and experienced staff gain a deep understanding of your background, challenges, and goals. Comprehensive programming, therapy, and education, equips your family with the necessary tools to support your loved ones’ recovery journey.

In our treatment programs for young adults, you’ll receive the right level of support at each stage of recovery. Whether you need high-support care, or a few hours of outpatient treatment a week, our expert team will collaborate with you to find what works best for you.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

“The amount of gratitude I have for this facility and the staff has no measure!”

“They were so friendly and knowledgeable always willing to help and adjust to the needs of the people in their care. If you need help do not hesitate you will not regret it!”

 

 

 

 
Mike B. 

Former Client
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Our Levels of Care

Access our full range of treatments for substance use disorders.

 Learn more about levels of care 

 

 

 

 
 
 

Medical Detox and Inpatient
 
 

Residential Treatment
 
 

Day Treatment (PHP)
 
 

Intensive Outpatient (IOP)
 

 

Medical Detox and Inpatient

5-21 days of 24/7 on-site medical supervision.

Our Medically-Assisted Detox and Inpatient Center offers private rooms and 24/7 medically supervised care to support a safe recovery from drugs and alcohol, followed by comprehensive treatment that addresses your physical, psychological, emotional, and spiritual needs.

 

 
 

 

Medical Detox Explained 

 

 

Residential Treatment

30-45 days of on-site 24/7 treatment.

We provide residential treatment centers (RTCs) for teens 13-17 (18 in MD if still in high school) and young adults 18-35, treating mental health challenges including anxiety, depression, trauma, and substance use.

Our teen RTCs treat adolescents in a serene setting with smaller group sizes and a focus on strengthening the family unit. The length of stay is 30-45 days. Our young adult RTC offers young adults a peaceful environment in which they can be treated for mental health challenges. Both RTCs create a distraction-free space for healing, helping clients return to daily life feeling more balanced and well.

 

 
 

 

Teen Residential Care Explained 

 

 

Day Treatment (PHP)

4 weeks of on-site day treatment.

Our Teen & Young Adult Day Treatment program, also known as Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP), is a highly structured level of care that offers five days of robust programming a week.

Our mental health and substance use programming helps clients to stabilize, begin to understand their mental health and/or addiction struggles, and heal from them. We strive to help our clients become more like the person they want to be, without using negative coping strategies or substances to get there.

 

 
 

 

PHP Explained 

 

 

Intensive Outpatient (IOP)

12 weeks of on-site or virtual treatment.

Our Teen & Young Adult IOP, or Intensive Outpatient Program, consists of 3-4 days of weekly programming that treats mental health challenges such as depression, trauma, anxiety, and substance use.

IOP includes individual therapy, group therapy, and family therapy. This flexible treatment option occurs in the evening hours so you can continue meeting your school or work responsibilities while receiving care.

 

 
 

 

What is IOP?

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
Take the Quiz

See if you or a loved one needs substance use or mental health support

Developed by Chief Clinical Officer Sarah Fletcher LPC

 

 
 Take the Quiz 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
In Network With Most Major Insurance

 Instantly Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

Find Hope at Sandstone Care

 “Sandstone helped me to dig deep… and it taught me how to have a relationship with myself and relationships with others again. I was able to take the mask off, and I don’t have to put it back on.” 

Watch Jesse detail how he took the leap to recover from drug use and find himself again. Discover the skills he learned during his time with Sandstone, including accountability for actions, self-sufficiency, and emotional regulation that allowed him to claim control over his life and stay sober.

By tailoring treatment to the unique needs of patients like Jesse, we create an environment that fosters growth, healing, and the development of a strong support network.

 

 
 

 

Jesse’s Recovery Story

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
As Seen in the Press

Our scientifically proven and evidence-based treatment process continues to earn national praise and foster relationships with the following:

 

 

 

 

 
 

iHeart Radio

Immanuel Jones shares his survival story from suicide attempt to suicide prevention tips, and helping young people cope with mental health issues.

 

 
 

Fox News

Erika Alhashmi LPC warns about the danger of teens self-diagnosing their mental health disorders on TikTok.

 

 
 

NBC

Peter King LCPC, explains how Sandstone Care provides age-specific, not gender-specific treatment for teens and adults in the LGBTQIA+ community.

 

 
 

CBS

Adrienne Washington LCPC, explains the negative impact of social media and provides tips for users and parents.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
You Have Questions. We Have Answers.

 Our goal is to provide the most helpful information. Please reach out to us at (888) 850-1890 if you have any additional questions. We are here to help in any way we can. 

 

 

 

 
 
 Collapse All -->
 Expand All -->
 
 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 
How much treatment do young adults need?

 
 
 

 
 The amount of treatment you will need varies depending on your individual needs. At Sandstone Care, we believe in treating you as a whole person instead of just treating your substance use disorder in isolation, which is why length of stay may vary. 

 Typically, you can expect the Young Adult IOP program to be 16 hours a week, from 8 to 12 weeks. 

 After treatment, you will also have the opportunity to join support groups and other aftercare modalities to maintain the momentum you found in our drug addiction recovery program. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
Do you use Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)?

 
 
 

 
Yes. Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT) is proven to be effective in treating substance use disorders, especially in combination with evidence-based therapy and counseling. Our personalized programming is designed to learn your needs and goals so we can choose the best course of treatment for your specific challenges. By offering medically-supervised withdrawal from substances, we help our clients recover quickly and safely from drug or alcohol use.

We also recognize that not all situations call for MAT. Our medical team will evaluate your circumstances and determine what type of treatment is appropriate for you. Learn more about Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) here .

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What are the other people in young adult rehabs like? 

 
 
 

 
The young adults at Sandstone Care are people who have been impacted by a variety of addiction and mental health issues. Typically they are struggling with or have struggled with anxiety, depression, trauma, and drug abuse.

The safety of our clients is our top priority, so individuals who exhibit behavioral issues that would disrupt the treatment milieu are not appropriate for Sandstone Care. This includes individuals with a history of violence, are actively suicidal or psychotic, or who have severe and persistent mental illness such as schizophrenia.

Each client is carefully screened by the admissions team and the Program Director before they enter the recovery center.

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
How do I know if I need detox/inpatient care?

 
 
 

 
 Knowing what level of care would be best for you is tricky! If you experience withdrawal symptoms when you attempt to discontinue use, it is more than likely you would benefit from detox. However, the best way to know whether or not you would benefit from medical detox is to get a professional opinion. Our trained admissions team would be happy to speak with you and advise you on next steps. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What is the most effective intervention for substance use?

 
 
 

 
 There are a variety of different effective approaches and strategies for providing treatment for substance abuse problems. 

 Early intervention can help reduce the harm of substances, reduce risk behaviors, and improve health and social function. Health care that is age group specific and involves family members is also proven to work faster and last longer than other addiction treatment programs. 

 The most common and effective interventions for substance abuse have been shown to include a form of behavioral health therapy in order for you to learn coping skills. 

 Examples of mental health treatments can include Cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), Dialectical behavioral therapy (DBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
Let’s Take the Next Steps Together

We understand taking the first step is difficult. There is no shame or guilt in asking for help or more information. We are here to support you in any way we can.

 

 
 Call (888) 850-1890 Verify Insurance 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 Verify Insurance (888) 850-1890

What is Substance Use and Mental Health Rehab Like For Teens" class="video-iframe"  data-src="https://player.vimeo.com/video/890456825?dnt=1&app_id=122963" width="640" height="360" frameborder="0" allow="autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin"></iframe></div></div><div class="l-video-text__col l-video-text__col--text list-item-column col-12 col-lg-6 order-2 order-lg-1"><div class="image-text__content"><h2>What is Teen Residential Treatment?</h2>
<p>Sandstone Care’s teen residential treatment centers offer a comprehensive 30-45 day program designed to help adolescents ages 13-17(18 in MD if still in high school) overcome substance use, addiction, and mental health disorders like trauma, anxiety, and depression.</p>
<p>Our accredited, evidence-based program offers personalized therapy, medication management, and 24/7 care from a compassionate, experienced team. We take a holistic approach to healing, helping teens develop life skills and emotional resilience, while also engaging families in the recovery process.</p>
<p>After treatment, our dedicated aftercare team ensures lasting success by connecting your teen with ongoing support and resources tailored to their unique journey.</p>
<p>We offer seamless step-down options like <a href="/teens/levels-of-care/day-treatment/">Day Treatment</a> and <a href="/teens/levels-of-care/intensive-outpatient-treatment/">Intensive Outpatient Programs</a>, providing ongoing support without the need to search for a new treatment center—saving you both time and money.</p>
</div></div></div>

                <h2><strong>Signs it’s Time to Seek Help</strong></h2>
<p style="text-align: left;">As a parent, it’s heartbreaking to see your teen struggling. You may notice them withdrawing, acting out, or sinking deeper into substance use or mental health challenges. When their world seems to be unraveling, and nothing you’ve tried seems to help, it may be time to consider residential mental health or addiction treatment.</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">You don’t have to face this alone. Residential treatment can give your teen the care they need to heal, and give your family the support to start fresh. Reach out today.</p>
<p style="text-align: left;">Signs that show it’s time for a program at one of our residential centers for youth include:</p>

        </div>
    </div>


                    <div class="l-columns__row row"><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 col-xl-5th"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-slim-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_MarijuanaTreatment_100422.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_MarijuanaTreatment_100422.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Substance Use</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">Substance use is out of control and affecting their health, relationships, or future.</p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 col-xl-5th"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-slim-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_DepressionTreatment_02_100522.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_DepressionTreatment_02_100522.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Mental Health Struggles</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">Mental health struggles, like anxiety, ADHD, OCD, or depression, are taking over their life.</p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 col-xl-5th"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-slim-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="Academic and Vocational Support" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AcademicAndVocationalSupport_093022.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_AcademicAndVocationalSupport_093022.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="Academic and Vocational Support" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>Decline in Schoolwork</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">School and daily responsibilities are slipping, and they’re losing interest in things they once loved.</p>
</div></article></div><div class="l-columns__col list-item-column list-item-column col-12 col-sm-6 col-md-4 col-xl-5th"><article class="icon_image blurb js-blurb blurb--card_column_style has-slim-padding "><div class='blurb__image-wrap blurb__image-wrap--icon_image'><img width="100" height="100" src="data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20100%20100'%3E%3C/svg%3E" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" data-lazy-src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_TraumaPTSDTreatment_02_100522.svg" /><noscript><img width="100" height="100" src="https://www.sandstonecare.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/1469415_Icons_TraumaPTSDTreatment_02_100522.svg" class="blurb__image" alt="No caption generated" /></noscript></div><h5 class='blurb__title'>You’re Scared for their Safety</h5><div class='blurb__content'><p style="text-align: left;">You’re scared for their safety, whether through risky behaviors, self-harm, or thoughts of suicide.</p>
