 Home Page / Care for Teens / Teen Treatment Programs / Teen Residential Treatment 
 
Residential Treatment Centers for Teens (RTCs)

A safe, supportive home-like environment where young people struggling with substance use and mental health challenges receive personalized, evidence-based care to foster lasting recovery & personal growth.

 
 

 

 
 

 

Treatment Overview

 

 

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
We’re here to help 24/7

 

 

 (888) 850-1890 Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Our Accreditations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

What is teen rehab?

 

What is Teen Residential Treatment?

Sandstone Care’s teen residential treatment centers offer a comprehensive 30-45 day program designed to help adolescents ages 13-17(18 in MD if still in high school) overcome substance use, addiction, and mental health disorders like trauma, anxiety, and depression.

Our accredited, evidence-based program offers personalized therapy, medication management, and 24/7 care from a compassionate, experienced team. We take a holistic approach to healing, helping teens develop life skills and emotional resilience, while also engaging families in the recovery process.

After treatment, our dedicated aftercare team ensures lasting success by connecting your teen with ongoing support and resources tailored to their unique journey.

We offer seamless step-down options like Day Treatment and Intensive Outpatient Programs , providing ongoing support without the need to search for a new treatment center—saving you both time and money.

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

60 – 90 Day Stay

 

Safe 24/7 Monitoring

 

Aftercare Planning

 

Large Insurance Network

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Teen Residential Treatment Centers

Our admissions team will help you find the right teenage treatment center for your child based on availability, length of stay, academic support needs, travel considerations, and your insurance provider. Explore our residential programs for teenagers below:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
Mental Health Residential Treatment Center for Teens
 
 
 864 Barranca Drive, Castle Rock, CO 80104, Castle Rock, Colorado, 80104

 (720) 782-0502 
 

 

 

 
 Age Groups: Teens 

 Levels of Care: Residential 

 Treatment Programs: Mental Health 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
Teen Drug Rehab in Maryland
 
 
 2020 Martins Grant Ct., Crownsville, Maryland, 21032

 (720) 782-0502 
 

 

 

 
 Age Groups: Teens 

 Levels of Care: Residential Assessments 

 Treatment Programs: Mental Health Substance Use Dual-Diagnosis 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
Teen Drug Rehab in Colorado
 
 
 5250 Pikes Peak Hwy, Cascade, Colorado, 80809

 (719) 249-2465 
 

 

 

 
 Age Groups: Teens 

 Levels of Care: Residential Assessments 

 Treatment Programs: Mental Health Substance Use Dual-Diagnosis 
 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 Signs it’s Time to Seek Help 

As a parent, it’s heartbreaking to see your teen struggling. You may notice them withdrawing, acting out, or sinking deeper into substance use or mental health challenges. When their world seems to be unraveling, and nothing you’ve tried seems to help, it may be time to consider residential mental health or addiction treatment.

You don’t have to face this alone. Residential treatment can give your teen the care they need to heal, and give your family the support to start fresh. Reach out today.

Signs that show it’s time for a program at one of our residential centers for youth include:

 

 

 

 

 

Substance Use

Substance use is out of control and affecting their health, relationships, or future.

 

Mental Health Struggles

Mental health struggles, like anxiety, ADHD, OCD, or depression, are taking over their life.

 

Decline in Schoolwork

School and daily responsibilities are slipping, and they’re losing interest in things they once loved.

 

You’re Scared for their Safety

You’re scared for their safety, whether through risky behaviors, self-harm, or thoughts of suicide.

 

 

Other treatment hasn’t worked

Previous therapies haven’t worked, and you feel like you’re running out of options.

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Getting Started is Easy!

 

 

 

 

 
 

Step 1: Call Us

Call and speak with an admissions coordinator

 

 
 

Step 2: Verify Insurance

Verify insurance or set up a financial plan

 

 
 

Step 3: Schedule an Assessment

Schedule a clinical assessment to start treatment

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
How We Are Different

At Sandstone Care, we’re dedicated to creating lasting, positive change for your loved one. We know that every teenager is unique, which is why we provide an individualized, compassionate inpatient treatment experience unlike any other. Whether your child is struggling with addiction, mental health issues, or both, we offer specialized treatment options tailored to their needs, helping them heal in a safe and supportive environment.

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Age-Specific Care

Addiction and mental health issues affect teens differently than adults. That’s why we customize our treatment services to the unique developmental needs of teens, including those who identify as LGBTQ, to ensure they receive the best care possible.

 

Evidence-Based Therapy

Our team of professionals uses scientifically proven, evidence-based therapies that deliver consistent and lasting recovery. We combine mental health services with addiction treatment to address co-occurring issues, providing a comprehensive approach to healing.

 

Small Group Sizes & Peer Support

Your loved one is more than just a number. We keep our group sizes small to provide focused mentoring, allowing your teen to build meaningful connections through peer support while receiving the personalized attention they need.

 

Individualized Treatment Plans

Every teen has their own story, goals, and challenges. We create individualized treatment plans designed around your child’s unique circumstances to give them the best chance at recovery and long-term success.

 

Academic and Vocational Support

In addition to comprehensive treatment, our residential program offers academic and vocational support. We ensure your teen continues their education and develops valuable life skills, helping them stay on track for future success while they focus on healing.

 

The Family Component

When one family member struggles with substance use or mental health, it affects everyone. Our family therapy and parent support groups help heal the entire family unit, giving everyone the tools they need to grow and recover together.

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

Would highly recommend for anyone with a teen struggling with substance abuse and mental health.

We have felt included and supported and are so grateful for the care they’ve provided to our son at such a vulnerable and scary time.

 

 

 

 
Cindy G.

Parent
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Program Highlights

 Our residential treatment for teens considers each child’s unique social, physical, and emotional development, so they can have the best chance at achieving a long-lasting recovery. 

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Small By Design

Each residential treatment facility for youth provides clinical environment with small group sizes and a max number of 22 patients.

 

Serene Environment

One of our residential treatment centers for teenagers is nestled peacefully in Pikes Peak Mountain in Colorado, and the other spans across six acres of wooded properties in central Maryland.

 

Length Of Stay

Teens typically stay 30-45 days and develop customized aftercare plans with their therapist to enter other programs or smoothly transition back to home life.

 

Family Focused

Family involvement is difficult and sometimes painful work, but we believe it is crucial for treatment to be effective. Family therapy is foundational to our residential treatment programs for youth.

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Teen Treatment Program Curriculum

Our residential treatment for youth centers around a 7-week curriculum of group process topics that help teens understand their challenges from all angles. This curriculum gives your child the knowledge, tools, and compassion needed for positive change.

 

 

 

 
 
 

Identity
 
 

Relationships
 
 

Recovery
 
 

Inner Work
 
 

DBT (Part 1)
 
 

DBT (Part 2)
 
 

Stress Reduction
 

 

Identity

 Week 1: Teens explore skills and topics that aid them in learning more about themselves and their recovery identities (as opposed to their substance using or poor mental health identities). They focus on introspective subjects like self-esteem, self-discovery, values & morals, attitudes & beliefs, and self-acceptance.

 

Relationships

 Week 2: Teens learn new social skills, including how to navigate and create healthy relationships with friends, family, partners, teachers, and other important people in their lives. They examine how they fit into these different relationships in a prosocial way, and focus on improving skills like communication, assertiveness, codependency, boundary setting, impulse control, compassion, and more.

 

Recovery

 Week 3: Teens develop positive coping skills like grounding techniques, while also focusing on subjects like the effects of substance use on the brain, grief & loss in addiction recovery, building resilience, coping with trauma, self-nurturing, and maintaining safety.

 

Inner Work

 Week 4: Teens learn skills that will help them handle life’s inevitable challenges. They explore topics like anger management, building trust in relationships, making commitments in recovery, and problem solving. They also learn how to assess triggers and cope with cravings in healthy ways.

 

DBT (Part 1)

 Week 5: Teens learn all about the first two branches of Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT): Mindfulness and Distress Tolerance. They explore related topics like mindfulness meditation, recognizing different states of mind, self-soothing, as well as WHAT skills, HOW skills, ACCEPTS skills, and PROMISE skills.

 

DBT (Part 2)

 Week 6: Teens finish up their lessons on Distress Tolerance and begin learning about the last two branches of Dialectical Behavior Therapy: Interpersonal Effectiveness and Emotional Regulation. They dive into related topics like accepting reality, goals and roadblocks, distortions and stories, mutual purpose and respect, DEAR MAN skills, smooth SAILING skills, and SERENITY skills.

 

Stress Reduction

 Week 7: Teens develop stress reduction skills this week and learn how to distinguish between good stress and bad stress. Using Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) skills, they begin to understand how stress affects the mind-body connection, appreciate what parts of life they can and can’t control, and learn helpful ways to cope with stress and painful situations.

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

The Obvious Choice for Effective Treatment

At Sandstone Care, we believe in personalized and specialized care. Unlike generic treatment approaches, we understand that each person’s circumstances are unique.

Using thorough assessments, our licensed therapists and medical professionals gain a deeper understanding of your child’s background, challenges, and goals. We then equip your teen and your family with the necessary tools to support your child’s recovery journey.

With our full continuum of care, your teenager will receive the right level of support at every stage. Whether they need high-support inpatient care at a teenage residential treatment center, or just a few hours of outpatient treatment a week, our expert team will find their best fit.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

 

What We Treat at Our Residential Facilities for Youth

In our teen residential program, we work on helping teens to uncover the root cause of their addiction, and then empower them to disrupt the cycle they are trapped in.

We specialize in helping teens navigate their use of:

 

Alcohol

Opioids

Marijuana

Stimulants

& More

 

We also understand that substance use and mental health conditions often occur together. One issue might feed off of the other, creating a vicious cycle. That’s why we make sure mental health conditions are addressed during teen treatment.

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

Getting Off the Couch

Teenagers grappling with mental health challenges and substance use often struggle to communicate, which can make traditional talk-therapy difficult for them.

That’s why our therapy is not the sofa-based, teeth-pulling kind. At our youth residential treatment centers, we use experiential therapy which includes dynamic activities to help your teenager find new ways to process their emotions.

Whether your teen resonates with exploring the outdoors, expressing themselves through art or music, or finding connection through equine therapy, our empathetic staff will help them find personalized healing that feels effective for them.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
In Network With Most Major Insurance

 Instantly Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

Kind hearted people willing to go the extra mile for not just the struggling teen but for the entire family.

 

 

 

 
Hans E.

Parent
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
Finding What Works Best for Your Teen

Every teen we serve is unique and responds differently to different therapies and activities, so we offer a wide range of treatment approaches in individual and group therapy settings.

Our licensed staff members use evidence-based practices equip teens and their families with the tools they need in order to make positive, healthy changes in their lives.

 Some of the treatments and therapies we use include: 

 Call (888) 850-1890   Verify Insurance 

 

 

 

 

 

Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT)

DBT equips your teen with practical skills to manage emotions and improve relationships.

 

Equine Therapy

Engaging with horses can promote emotional growth, confidence, self-awareness, and other crucial life skills for your teen.

 

Music Therapy

Utilizing music as a tool, your teen can learn to express their emotions healthily.

 

Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)

CBT guides your teen in identifying and changing negative thoughts and behaviors.

 

Acceptance Commitment Therapy (ACT)

This mindfulness-based therapy helps your teen accept thoughts and feelings to build a resilient mindset.

 

Wilderness Therapy

Engaging in outdoor experiences can promote self-reflection, connection, and teamwork for your child.

 

Art Therapy

Through painting, sketching, and other types of art, your teen can explore their emotions while reducing stress.

 
 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

Arriving at Our Residential Treatment Centers for Youth

When you arrive at one of our residential treatment centers for adolescents, you’ll be greeted by our friendly staff and introduced to the program director, therapist, and a member of our healthcare team. We will take some time to talk with you as a family about the residential program for youth and answer your questions.

After meeting with the family, your teen will have a complete initial evaluation with our primary therapist. This comprehensive bio-psychosocial assessment reviews mental illness history, family dynamics, medications, behavioral issues, and academic status.

Following completion of the assessment, your teen will then settle into their bedroom, meet the other residential treatment teens, and explore the lodge. In total, the intake and assessment time will take about two hours.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 
 

 

 
We’re available 7 days a week to help answer any questions you may have.

 

 
 Call (888) 850-1890 Verify Insurance 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 

 

Your Care Team

In our residential treatment program for teens, our team members are more than just clinicians – they’re mentors. Beyond providing expert therapeutic insights, they will actively engage with your child on a personal level.

Each resident is assigned a multidisciplinary team, which includes a psychiatrist, individual therapist, family therapist, nurses, teacher, and recovery counselors to help them succeed.

These mentors play a crucial role in helping your teen to build support systems and learning valuable life lessons. By acting as positive role models, they provide your teenager with accountability, a listening ear, and a compassionate perspective to help them picture what their future could look like after treatment.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 
The Continuum of Care

Care for Wherever You Are in Your Journey

Access a full range of treatments for mental health and substance use disorders. Whether you need a safe transitional living community, inpatient care, or outpatient therapy, we have a program to help.

 Learn more about levels of care 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 Teen Residential Treatment
 
 

 Teen Day Treatment (PHP)
 
 

 Teen After School Intensive Outpatient (IOP)
 
 

Virtual IOP
 

 
 

 Teen Residential Treatment

30-45 days of on-site 24/7 treatment.

Our adolescent residential treatment centers are designed specifically for teens struggling with mental health issues (such as anxiety, depression, bipolar disorder, etc), substance use, and co-occurring mental health disorders.

We help teens learn a healthy way to handle the daily challenges of their lives. We provide an intimate serene environment with smaller group sizes and a focus on strengthening the family unit. We provide academic support by certified teachers to ensure clients stay up to date on their coursework.

 

 
 

 Teen Day Treatment (PHP)

2-4 weeks of on-site day treatment.

Our Teen Day Treatment Program, also known as Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP), is a highly structured level of care for teens that offers five days of robust programming a week.

Our two distinct mental health and substance use tracks help teens to stabilize, begin to understand their mental health and/or addiction struggles, and find lasting wellness. We strive to help our clients become more like the person they want to be, without using negative coping strategies or substances to get there.

 

 
 

 Teen After School Intensive Outpatient (IOP)

8-12 weeks of on-site or virtual treatment.

Our Teen IOP, or Intensive Outpatient Program, offers two distinct tracks to address teen needs, each track consisting of 3-4 days of weekly programming.

Through our mood disorder track we are able to focus on mental health, depression, trauma, and anxiety. In our dual diagnosis track we are able to support teens with substance use and mental health challenges. Each focuses on developing positive social and academic habits while continuing with their school responsibilities.

 

 
 

Virtual IOP

8-12 weeks of virtual treatment.

Virtual IOP offers the same programming as in-person IOP, except it’s all online. This is ideal for those who live too far to drive in, have transportation issues, or have health concerns that make in-person treatment challenging.

 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 You Have Questions. We Have Answers. 

 Our goal is to provide the most helpful information. Please reach out to us  at (888) 850-1890 if you have any additional questions. We are here to help in any way we can. 

 

 

 

 
 
 Collapse All -->
 Expand All -->
 
 
 

 

 
 

 

 
 
Who provides treatment at youth residential treatment centers?

 
 
 

 
 Each individual’s treatment team consists of six experts who specialize in teen trauma, substance use, and teen mental health treatment including our program director, family therapist, primary therapist, psychiatrist, teacher, and medical director (supported by our psychiatric nurse practitioner). Everyone on the treatment team is dedicated to supporting your teen’s wellbeing. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What’s the academic program like at residential treatment centers for youth?

 
 
 

 
 Our residential academic program is scheduled for two hours per day, five days a week. The program is led by a certified teacher who works in collaboration with the client’s home school to obtain current classwork to help keep your teen current in his or her schoolwork. In addition to our addiction and behavioral health services, Sandstone Care has partnered with LearnWell Academy to provide our comprehensive education services to ensure your teen stays on track. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What’s the difference between Inpatient Rehab and a Teenage Residential Treatment Center?

 
 
 

 
 Residential centers are rehabilitation services that are provided outside of a hospital setting and typically offer longer stays within the facility. These live-in centers provide structure, assistance with employment or education, as well as group and individual therapies that focus on addressing underlying psychological or behavioral problems related to substance abuse. 

 Inpatient rehabilitation often involves a short-term stay within a hospital setting with health professionals and is used in emergency situations to help stabilize their health. Teens in those situations often continue treatment in follow-up outpatient programs at teen treatment centers. Please note that our centers do not currently treat eating disorders . 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What are the sleeping arrangements like?

 
 
 

 
 At our teen residential treatment centers, teens are provided with semi-private, comfortable bedrooms with beautiful views. We balance privacy and safety, to make sure that every teen in treatment can heal in a secure, supportive, and structured environment. 

 Sleeping arrangements in our residential treatment facilities are designed to facilitate a structured schedule. By following this routine, we help adolescents avoid the challenges associated with being exhausted, which helps them to engage at their best during therapy and activities. 

 Open communication is crucial, and during family sessions, you’ll have the opportunity to discuss your teen’s sleep schedule and habits. We are committed to working together to ensure the best possible outcomes for your teen’s journey. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What are the benefits of residential programs for teenagers?

 
 
 

 
 When you enroll your child in residential treatment for teens, you not only help them recover from substance use and mental health issues, you are giving them the opportunity to get the skills they need to lead a fulfilling life. Some of the benefits that your teen will enjoy from adolescent behavioral residential treatment include: 

 

 Structure and routine with 24/7 support 

 Individualized therapies to help them gain healthy coping skills for their specific needs and goals 

 Stronger family connections from family therapy sessions 

 One-on-one mentorship from our team leaders who set strong examples for the future 

 Healthy relationships with other teens who truly understand what they are going through 

 Support to get back on track with their schoolwork 

 A guided transition back home to continue healing long after treatment 

 

 Between our intentional, thoughtful programming and certified, compassionate staff, our juvenile residential treatment centers will have your teenager feeling like themselves again in no time. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What is a RTC in mental health?

 
 
 

 
 An RTC, or Residential Treatment Center , is a live-in facility that provides intensive mental health care for individuals, often adolescents, who need structured support. In an RTC, individuals receive therapy, counseling, and medical care for mental health disorders such as depression, anxiety, trauma, or behavioral issues. The goal is to provide a safe and supportive environment where individuals can focus on healing and developing coping skills before transitioning back to everyday life. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What is another name for a residential treatment facility?

 
 
 

 
 A Residential Treatment Center (RTC), also known as a residential treatment facility, residential program, or rehab center, provides 24/7 care for adolescents, teens, and young adults struggling with mental health and substance use challenges. In a safe and structured environment away from home, they receive individualized therapy, medical support, and life skills development to promote lasting recovery and personal growth, all while building healthy coping mechanisms to face the challenges of everyday life. 

 Other names for a residential treatment facility include residential behavioral health programs , youth residential mental health facilities , rehabilitation centers , inpatient treatment centers , and therapeutic boarding schools (for adolescents). 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What is RTC in school?

 
 
 

 
 The goal of an RTC in school is to help students manage their behavior, receive counseling or therapy, and reintegrate into the regular classroom setting once they’re ready to continue learning in a positive environment. 

 

 

 

 
 

 

 
 
What does RTC stand for in rehab?

 
 
 

 
 In rehab, RTC stands for Residential Treatment Center . It refers to a facility where individuals, often struggling with substance use or mental health disorders, live on-site and receive 24/7 care, including therapy, counseling, and medical support. The goal of an RTC in rehab is to provide a structured, supportive environment to help individuals achieve long-term recovery and personal growth.

We understand taking the first step is difficult. There is no shame or guilt in asking for more information. We are here to support you in any way we can.

rawMentions: Substance Use for Adults Explore individualized treatment programs that help adults with drug abuse, detox, and rehab.
Get stable fast with detox programs and personalized care designed to empower adults to break entrenched patterns.
Whether you’re the parent of a teen who needs treatment, a young adult exploring outpatient options, or an adult in need of detox, you’re guaranteed to find what you need at one of our facilities.
Whether you need detox or outpatient treatment, our continuum of care will meet you wherever you are in the recovery journey.
Adult Treatment Centers: Drug & Alcohol Detox
Whether you are nervous about stepping into detox or wondering, “How can I find a drug rehab for adults near me?
With individualized treatment programs, small program sizes, and dual diagnosis treatments, your detox journey will make sure you are empowered to reclaim every part of your life.
Medical Detox and Inpatient

Our Medically-Assisted Detox and Inpatient Center offers private rooms and 24/7 medically supervised care to support a safe recovery from drugs and alcohol, followed by comprehensive treatment that addresses your physical, psychological, emotional, and spiritual needs.
Medical Detox Explained
Detox
Colorado Springs Detox Center
Virginia Detox Center
Medication-Assisted Detox (MAD) vs.
Many treatment centers claim to offer Medication-Assisted Treatment to clients, but only provide Medication-Assisted Detox.
Sandstone Care offers both Medication-Assisted Treatment and Medication-Assisted Detox to clients in need.
Medication-Assisted Detox is a short-term treatment given to all clients who present with substance use disorder.
Whether you need a safe detox program, inpatient care, or outpatient therapy, we have a program to help.
Detox Explained
Explore individualized treatment programs that help you with drug and alcohol use through detox and recovery.
Levels of Care: Detox Residential
Your medical team will see you through detox and stabilization, providing everything you need to succeed.
Whether you need high-support inpatient treatment, or residential treatment after detox, our expert team will collaborate with you to find your best fit.
Detox is our only level of care for adults at this time.
How do I know if I need detox?
In this case, you would benefit from the detox healthcare services at one of our inpatient drug and alcohol rehab centers.
However, the best way to know whether or not you would benefit from medical detox is to get a professional opinion.
How long is medical detox?
When you arrive to the Sandstone Care Detox & Inpatient Center, you will be greeted at the door by one of our staff members.
Do you use medications to assist with detox?
They will assess your situation and determine which prescription drugs will be used to make your detox safe and comfortable.
Please note that Sandstone Care’s Detox locations are not methadone clinics.
Is family allowed at the detox facility?
No, family members will not allowed in the detox facility during your treatment, and we do not provide family therapy at the detox level of care.
However, family members and friends are welcome to call our Detox Center to receive an update about their loved one.
During your stay at one of our detox centers, you will have access to your personal cell phone.
You can begin the process of getting treated at one of our Detox Centers by calling our admissions department.
Please note that we do not offer medication-assisted treatment (MAT), which is typically reserved for detox settings.
How do I know if I need detox/inpatient care?
If you experience withdrawal symptoms when you attempt to discontinue use, it is more than likely you would benefit from detox.

rawMentions: Now accepting clients ages 18–35 for PHP/IOP services and at The Alps Residential Treatment Center (RTC).

Questions About Residential Treatment?
How do I know if my teen needs residential addiction or mental health treatment?

If your teen is still struggling to manage their mental health despite outpatient therapy or medication, or if their ability to function day-to-day is significantly impacted, residential treatment may be the next step toward healing.

Some signs that residential care may be appropriate include difficulty maintaining relationships, falling behind at school, persistent feelings of hopelessness, thoughts of self-harm, or suicidal ideation.
At our Residential Treatment facility in Colorado, we provide comprehensive support for both addiction and mental health care in a structured, teen-focused setting.
The best way to determine whether residential care is right for your teen is to consult with a mental health professional.
What should my teen bring to the Residential Treatment Program in Castle Rock, Colorado?
Yes, we offer medication management services as part of our Castle Rock Residential Treatment Program .
We’ll walk you through every step of getting started with our Teen Residential Treatment Program , answer your questions, and help determine if it’s the right fit for your teen.

What is another name for a residential treatment facility?

A Residential Treatment Center (RTC), also known as a residential treatment facility, residential program, or rehab center, provides 24/7 care for adolescents, teens, and young adults struggling with mental health and substance use challenges.

Other names for a residential treatment facility include residential behavioral health programs , youth residential mental health facilities , rehabilitation centers , inpatient treatment centers , and therapeutic boarding schools (for adolescents).
In rehab, RTC stands for Residential Treatment Center .

Teen Day Treatment (PHP)
In contrast with our residential program, teens in adolescent PHP treatment live at home and attend their treatment during the day.
Our flexible teen IOP, or intensive outpatient program, is a step down from our PHP program and can be completed after school.
Does my child really need PHP?
Intensive Emotional or Behavioral Struggles: If your child is experiencing significant emotional or behavioral challenges that affect their daily life, PHP may offer the structured support they need.

Difficulty Managing Daily Responsibilities: If your child is finding it hard to cope with school, relationships, or daily responsibilities due to their mental health, PHP can provide more thorough interventions than afterschool programs.
Substance Use: If you are concerned about your teen’s substance use alongside their emotional or behavioral challenges, PHP can address both aspects at the same time.
Increased Isolation or Withdrawal: If you’ve noticed your child becoming more withdrawn or isolated, PHP can provide a supportive environment for them to connect with peers and professionals.
What does PHP stand for in rehab?
What should my teen expect in PHP?
Teen PHP ensures that your teen won’t have to put their life on hold to get the treatment they need.

rawMentions: Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT) | Sandstone Care
Home Page / Treatments Available at Sandstone Care / Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)
Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)
What Is Medication-Assisted Treatment?
Those who struggle with alcohol use disorder or opioid use disorder are primarily the recipients of Medication-Assisted Treatment.
Medication-Assisted Treatment Explained
Medication-Assisted Treatment couples psychiatric care with a highly structured medication regimen to support healing from the inside out.
That is why Sandstone Care offers Medication-Assisted Treatment to clients at any of our program locations and treatment levels.
Many treatment centers claim to offer Medication-Assisted Treatment to clients, but only provide Medication-Assisted Detox.
Sandstone Care offers both Medication-Assisted Treatment and Medication-Assisted Detox to clients in need.
Medication-Assisted Treatment, on the other hand, is utilized throughout the continuum of care at Sandstone Care.
Medication Assisted Treatment For Alcohol Use Disorder
Medication Assisted Treatment For Opioid Addiction
Sandstone Care utilizes MAT to help you remain substance free when entering treatment and throughout your recovery.
MAT involves the use of prescription drugs designed to help in:
MAT works best when coupled with treatment programs like those offered at Sandstone Care, where a focus is put on co-occurring mental disorders in addition to drug or opioid addiction.
Signs You’d Benefit From MAT
MAT helps to manage or eliminate cravings before they start, making your recovery journey that much easier.
Why Choose MAT?
Medications used in MAT work to control addiction within the limbic system, while other aspects of therapy support the rest of the body and mind.

While the medication prescribed through MAT can solve physical problems for a client during rehabilitation and recovery, it cannot always solve the behavioral and mental issues found at the root of addiction.
It is important to remember that MAT does not work alone – clients receiving MAT should commit to all aspects of treatment in order to overcome addiction.
MAT is a long-term maintenance therapy, coupled with intensive psychiatric treatment.
MAT is only used with clients who have opioid or alcohol use disorder.
A methadone clinic dispenses medications and provides support when using MAT for opioid use disorder.
If you use methadone for MAT, you need to go to a methadone clinic each day to get your daily dose.
How Sandstone Care’s Clinical Framework Supports MAT
What is Suboxone (Buprenorphine)?
Buprenorphine ( Suboxone and Subutex are common brands)
Naltrexone ( Vivitrol and Revia are common brands)
What us Naltrexone (Vivitrol)?
Yes, we do utilize medication-assisted treatment at Sandstone Care.
Please note that we do not offer medication-assisted treatment (MAT), which is typically reserved for detox settings.
Do you use Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)?
Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT) is proven to be effective in treating substance use disorders, especially in combination with evidence-based therapy and counseling.
Learn more about Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) here .
We also recognize that not all situations call for MAT.

evidence.label: program:sober_living
evidence.snippet: Do you provide IOP, PHP, or sober living levels of care for adults?

rawMentions: IOP includes individual therapy, group therapy, and family therapy.
In PHP, teens participate in various therapeutic treatments, including group clinical programming and bi-weekly individual and family therapy.
We provide family therapy and parent support groups to help heal the whole family.
No, family members will not allowed in the detox facility during your treatment, and we do not provide family therapy at the detox level of care.
Family involvement is critical to recovery, which is why our programming includes regular family therapy .

Our Residential Treatment Program offers a balance of therapeutic structure and engaging experiences for young people, including recreational therapy, experiential therapies, and ongoing family therapy to strengthen support at home.
Family Therapy
In family therapy, a therapist helps family members recognize the roles they play within emotional family dynamics, and learn how they can best support your teen.
That’s why we offer individual family therapy sessions every other week.
We also offer weekly Multi- Family therapy groups to reinforce key skills, strengthen communication, and give families a source of guidance and support throughout the treatment journey.
Our family therapy and parent support groups help heal the entire family unit, giving everyone the tools they need to grow and recover together.
Family therapy is foundational to our residential treatment programs for youth.
Stronger family connections from family therapy sessions
